scope_summary: Investigate the e2e/06-claim-actions.spec.ts:54 (AI decision recording) failure that blocked the commit/push gate. Reproduce on current DB, classify, no product fix. Result: failure does not reproduce; 3 green runs (isolated x2 + full suite 89/89); DB accumulation grew yet all passed, ruling out row-volume; cause is a transient timing flake against a tight 15s test timeout.

e2e_repro.target_failure: e2e/06-claim-actions.spec.ts:54 (AI analysis runs + AI decision recorded with source=AI)

db_accumulation_evidence.interpretation: accumulation grew across the 3 runs yet 100% pass — row-volume ruled out as the cause; also modest sizes where a sort/scan is sub-millisecond

diagnosis.primary_root_cause: The 2026-05-28 88/89 was a transient timing flake: POST /api/claims/CLM-1006/ai-decision round-trip occasionally exceeded the test's tight 15s actionTimeout under machine load during a long serial run, leaving the button in 'Збереження…'. Not a product defect; not DB-row-volume.
evidence_file_lines: e2e/06-claim-actions.spec.ts:72-74 — ai-decision-recorded assertion {timeout:15000} vs analysis-completion {timeout:30000} at :61-64
PersistenceAuditCostService.cs:45-101 — IDbContextFactory + await using; no leak; outbox idempotency FirstOrDefaultAsync at :82-83 (negligible at 203 rows)
PersistenceAiAnalysisOrchestrator.cs:99,221 — IDbContextFactory + await using; GetLatestAsync:228 non-sargable OrderByDescending(CreatedAtUtc ?? DateTime.MinValue) (negligible at 57 rows)
HybridClaimReadService.cs:61-62 — CLM-1006 served from in-memory seed; the sync-over-async .GetAwaiter().GetResult() bridge is NOT on this failing path
Program.cs:31,61-63 — DbContexts lazy-connect; no startup migrate/seed
diagnosis.network_observations: backend logs show EF DbCommand 34-40ms; no errors; all flows completed
diagnosis.console_observations: AI provider resolved to: Mock (default safe fallback) — no DeepSeek/real external call

limitations: Single-machine, single-day timing evidence; a load-dependent flake's non-reproduction today does not prove it can never recur — hence the timeout-hardening recommendation
DB reset deliberately not performed (justified); fresh-DB behavior inferred from the accumulated-DB isolated pass, not directly executed
Single-browser (Chromium) coverage per the suite's design
